rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Stay Of Enforcement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY'S Motion For Stay Of Enforcement. Continued to September 28, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 16, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BOURGES VS. DANY SCHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Of March 21, 2014 On Grounds Of Defendant Misconduct
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Of March 21, 2014 On Grounds Of Defendant Misconduct. The motion is denied. There is no good cause to vacate the judgment and the court has already denied the instant motion on multiple occasions. Plaintiff should seek appellate relief and no longer file this repetitive motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ADAMS ET AL VS. MANJULA PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Joe Thompson And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MANJULABEN PATEL's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Joe Thompson And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. All objections are waived. Plaintiff to serve verified responses to (1) Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One and Request for Production of Documents Set One by October 9, 2015. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $600. Check to be made payable to Manjula Patel and delivered to the Law Offices of Bennett, Samuelsen, Reynolds, Allard, Cowperthwaite & Gelini no later than October 9, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com ,a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY VS. ARTISAN CONFECTIONS COMPAN, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT ARTISAN CONFECTIONS COMPANY, DAGOBA ORGANIC CHOCOLATES LLC'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Defendants' Motion to Quash Service of Summons is DENIED. Where the degree of control exercise by a parent corporate entity is pervasive and continual, the parent may be deemed subject to local jurisdiction for acts of its subsidiary under agency principals. (Rollins Burdick Hunder of Southern California, Inc. v. Alexander Service, Inc. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1, 9.) The actions and contacts of a parent corporate entity likewise may be attributable to a subsidiary for purposes of personal jurisdiction. (See Mathes v. National Utility Helicopters, Ltd. (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 182.) Provided that D Artisan retains intellectual property rights over D Dagoba chocolate, and the Dagoba chocolate that allegedly contains cadmium is sold in San Francisco stores - and has a retail website that provides a local San Francisco phone number and address for customer service - this court's exercise of personal jurisdiction over Defendants is proper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ALAIN BRAUX VS. COINBASE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Coinbase, Inc. (1) To Compel Arbitration; And (2) To Stay This Action Pending The Completion Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'S Motion Of Coinbase, Inc. (1) To Compel Arbitration; And (2) To Stay This Action Pending The Completion Thereof. Defendant Coinbase's Motion to Compel Arbitration and Stay Action is GRANTED IN PART. This Court holds as substantively unconscionable Section 9.1 of Defendant Coinbase's Revised User Agreement, which establishes that Defendant Coinbase cannot be held liable for "lost profits, special damages, incidental damages, or consequential damages . . . however arising, including negligence." (Decl. of Adrian MacNeil, Ex. C, Section 9.1) This provision is substantively unconscionable under Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 127. "Bitcoin exchanges are targets for hackers and thieves, and are often operated dishonestly and openly operate with security holes amounting to negligence." (Nicholas Godlove, J.D., Regulatory Overview of Virtual Currency (2014) 10 Okla. J. L. & Tech. 70.) A significantly large portion of bitcoins are stolen. Provided that the risk of theft and mischief is extraordinary at Coinbase, as Defendant concedes, it offends public policy to allow Defendant to contract out of liability for negligence. The dangers of unregulated virtual stock exchanges "make it all the more necessary that negligence be discouraged." (Bisso v. Inland Waterways Corp. (1955) 349 U.S. 85, 91.) Plaintiff will experience impermissible injustice if forced to submit his negligence claim to arbitration. Defendant's assertion that Plaintiff assumed the risk of $186,000 in theft is legally unfounded and further supports this Courts' position that the interests of justice require judicial resolution of Plaintiff's negligence claim. The California Supreme Court establishes that it is possible to sever unconscionable portions of an agreement and therefore find the agreement unenforceable in its entirety. (Armendariz, supra, 24 Cal.4th at p. 128.) The remaining provisions of Defendant Coinbase's Revised User Agreement, including the arbitration provision, nonetheless, are enforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 11, PLAINTIFF DANNY MEREDITH'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELIQUE G BRUNNER ET AL VS. DEBORAH SHARGEL ET AL (pltf 170.6 challenge of Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 16, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ANGELIQUE BRUNNER, RACHEL ROBASCIOTTI'S Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction. The motion is granted in part. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate a basis to compel defendant to make additional payments. The parties' dispute is fundamentally about money and what type of offset should exist. That issue can be resolved at the end of the partition action. There is no evidence regarding the financial condition of the parties or whether irreparable harm exists. There is no showing that the property is about to go into default. The court enjoins both parties from encumbering the property without further order of the court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
